Add batched deleteAllFiles internal mutation (#102)

## Summary
- Adds `convex/deleteAllFiles.ts` with a batched internal mutation that
deletes all Convex storage files
- Processes 1000 files per batch and self-schedules until all files are
deleted
- Intended as a temporary utility for cleaning up storage in
preview/prod deployments

## Usage
```bash
npx convex run --preview-name <name> --push deleteAllFiles:run
npx convex run --prod --push deleteAllFiles:run
```

## Test plan
- [ ] Deploy to preview and verify it deletes all storage files
- [ ] Remove the file after cleanup is complete

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Require semantic confirmation for structural pairs + u/v normalization fix

- Semantic gate: structural fingerprint pairs now require cosine >= 0.575
  from the semantic channel (main or recovery) to appear in results.
  Reduces noise from 48 to ~8 pairs for Geo 3 × DRN 6 while preserving
  the Thomas tricolon (rank 107, cosine 0.5787).
- Tighten fingerprint rarity filter: MAX_FINGERPRINT_FREQ 4 → 2
- Raise semantic recovery threshold: 0.55 → 0.575
- U/V normalization in synonym_dict: canonical-form grouping merges
  47 fragmented entry pairs (303 broken synonym links), e.g.
  ualidus/validus, uia/via now share all synonyms
- Add dictionary entries: lemma variant bridges (section 52) and
  synonym gaps from benchmark analysis (section 53)
- About page: add J.P. Koenig as Tesserae co-founder (2008)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: tesserae-v6
repository.description: Tesserae v6

commit.message:
Restore decode throughput: 83.9 → 92.3 tok/s (+10%)

The online softmax attention kernel (commit 02797e2) fixed the 262k context
crash but caused a throughput regression from 94.9 to 83.9 tok/s. This commit
recovers most of the loss with three changes:

1. Dual-path attention decode kernel (attention.cu):
   - Restored fast shared-memory kernel for contexts ≤12K tokens (48KB smem)
   - Falls back to online softmax kernel for larger contexts (O(1) smem)
   - Kernel selection based on max_kv_len at startup, compatible with CUDA graph
   - Attention kernel time: 0.87ms → 0.30ms/tok (matching original 0.29ms)

2. GPU-accelerated sampling (sampling.cu):
   - Temperature >0 previously downloaded all 248K logits (1MB) to CPU every token
   - New path: GPU reduces 248K logits to 1024 candidates, downloads only 8KB
   - CPU does top-k + softmax + sampling on the 1024 candidates
   - Eliminates cudaStreamSynchronize + 1MB D2H transfer per token
   - Temperature sampling now matches greedy speed (92.3 vs 92.4 tok/s)

3. Minor decode loop optimization (main.cu):
   - Defer tokenizer.decode() to only when callback is present
   - Cache getenv("NO_GRAPH") in static variable

Results (128 tokens, RTX 5090):
  Before: 83.9 tok/s (both greedy and temperature=0.8)
  After:  92.4 tok/s greedy, 92.3 tok/s temperature=0.8
  Original (pre-262k): 94.9 tok/s greedy

Remaining 2.6% gap from original is from binary layout effects due to added
code (prompt caching, chunked prefill, server support). All features preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: все даты в МСК, ручной /post всегда публикует, честный feedback

Корень проблемы: бот считал даты в UTC, а cron работал по МСК.
В 02:00 МСК (23:00 UTC) /search создавал сессии на "завтра по UTC",
а /post искал "сегодня по UTC" — разные даты, ничего не публиковалось.

Изменения:
- dates.ts: единый модуль todayMsk/tomorrowMsk для всех дат
- poster.ts: postVideoToChannel принимает {force: true}, возвращает PostResult
- index.ts: ручной /post всегда force=true (без проверки wasPostedToday),
  показывает что именно опубликовано/пропущено/не найдено
- scheduler.ts: автоматический cron оставляет force=false (защита от дублей)

commit.message:
Integrate Node-RED (CySOAR) as embedded npm module with portal auth gate

- server.cjs: new Express server that embeds node-red as an npm module
  at /node-red/, protected by a session-cookie auth middleware that calls
  Flask /api/auth/verify before every request; unauthenticated requests
  are redirected to the portal login page
- package.json: add express, node-red, cookie-parser runtime deps and
  a "start" script (node server.cjs)
- vite.config.js: dev proxy for /node-red/ (with WebSocket) → port 3001
  and /api /auth /oidc → Flask port 5252
- App.jsx: mark cysoar module as embedded:true so the sidebar tab calls
  setActiveTab instead of navigating externally; render a full-height
  <iframe src="/node-red/"> when the CySOAR tab is active
- .gitignore: exclude Node-RED credential file and lib directory

fix(newline-between-instructions): accept comments as separators between same-type instructions (#240)

## Summary

- Fix false positive in `tally/newline-between-instructions` (grouped
mode) where a comment between two same-type instructions (e.g. two
`ENV`s) was incorrectly flagged as "unexpected blank line"
- Comments between same-type instructions are intentional separators and
the rule now skips the grouping check when `PrevComment` is present
- Applied the same fix to the `--fix` resolver so it doesn't incorrectly
remove blank lines in this scenario

**Before (false positive):**
```dockerfile
# Add Julia to PATH and use bundled libraries (including libcurl)
ENV PATH=/usr/local/julia/bin:$PATH \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/julia/lib/julia

# Target x86_64 with haswell optimizations (AWS Lambda baseline) and generic fallback
ENV JULIA_CPU_TARGET="haswell,clone_all;generic"
```
```
💅 unexpected blank line between ENV and ENV
```

**After:** No violation.

## Test plan

- [x] Updated existing unit test ("grouped - same type with comment and
blank lines") to expect 0 violations
- [x] Added new test case matching the reported scenario (multiline ENV
with comment separator)
- [x] Updated snapshots for `TestFixRealWorldMetalama` and
`TestFixWindowsContainer`
- [x] All `internal/rules/tally`, `internal/fix`, and
`internal/integration` tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
* Treat comments between identical instructions as intentional
separators to avoid spurious newline/spacing violations.

* **Packaging / Containers**
* Improved container build snapshots and workflows: added .NET
runtime/SDK handling, persistent PATH updates, build tool installation,
working-directory/setup steps, cleanup, and a startup/entrypoint for
Windows images.

* **Tests**
* Added regression tests to cover same-type instruction sequences
separated by comments.

commit.message:
fix: coerce null repos.dev/clone to empty arrays in template config schema (#339)

YAML parses `dev:` and `clone:` with no value as `null`, not `undefined`.
The Zod schema used `.optional().default([])` which only handles `undefined`.
Add `.nullable().transform(v => v ?? [])` to coerce `null` to `[]`.

Fixes orchestrator crash: "Expected array, received null" at repos.dev/clone
when loading external project configs with empty dev/clone sections.

Co-authored-by: christrudelpw <chris@generacy.ai>

fix: pin avatar proxy to hnd1 region — exchange CDNs geo-blocked from US

The /api/avatar proxy was running in US regions (sfo1) where exchange
CDN domains (Binance, Bybit, MEXC, etc.) are geo-blocked, returning
500 errors. Pin to hnd1 (Tokyo) where exchanges are accessible.

Also adds 10s fetch timeout to prevent function hangs on slow CDNs.

commit.message:
fix(team): resolve worktree merge failures from dirty git state

- Track framework files (.ralph/, .claude/) before creating worktrees
  to prevent "untracked working tree files would be overwritten" errors
- Add ensureCleanWorkingTree() to stash or reset dirty state before
  each merge (handles vitest cache, failed rebase/merge leftovers)
- Detect "unmerged files" as a conflict condition for proper retry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(avatar): remove overflow clipping + fix glass modifier ordering

Two root causes preventing glass morph from working:

JS side (settings.tsx):
- animatedParentStyle had `overflow: 'hidden'` ALWAYS set, which maps to
  clipsToBounds=true on the CALayer. This clips the glass morph animation
  as it travels between positions, causing fallback to default scale+opacity.
  Now only clips when edit-menu is active (parentScale < 1).

Swift side (NativeProfileAvatarModule.swift):
- .frame() and .padding() were applied AFTER .glassEffectID(), breaking the
  glass shape geometry tracking. Apple's pattern requires glassEffect() and
  glassEffectID() to be the LAST modifiers on each view.
- Restructured from ZStack+padding to VStack+Spacers so layout positions
  the glass shapes naturally, with no modifiers after glassEffectID().

commit.message:
Fix similar view broken by negative bbox coordinates

bboxToPathParam joined coords with '-' and renderSimilar converted
dashes back to commas. Face detections near image edges can have
negative coordinates, causing the minus sign to be replaced as a
separator (e.g. y=-68 → "1854--68-2616-291" → "1854,,68,2616,291").

Switch the path-param separator to '_', which cannot appear in
signed integers.  Also fix the matching split in the PATCH
/api/faces/{md5}/{bbox} endpoint for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
